warnings.title: Epsilon Characterization Of Supremum
warnings.message: Extracted theorem/definition-like environment has no explicit title. The converter used a humanized label fallback, but the source should add an environment title for stable extraction and readable graph labels.
warnings.label: def:epsilon-characterization-of-supremum
warnings.excerpt: Let $A \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ and let $s \in \mathbb{R}$. The element $s$ is said to satisfy the \emph{epsilon characterization of the supremum of $A$} if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $a \in A$ such that \[ s-\varepsilon < a. \]

warnings.title: Epsilon Characterization Of Infimum
warnings.message: Extracted theorem/definition-like environment has no explicit title. The converter used a humanized label fallback, but the source should add an environment title for stable extraction and readable graph labels.
warnings.label: def:epsilon-characterization-of-infimum
warnings.excerpt: Let $A \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ and let $t \in \mathbb{R}$. The element $t$ is said to satisfy the \emph{epsilon characterization of the infimum of $A$} if $t$ is a lower bound of $A$ and for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $a \in A$ such that \[ a < t+\varepsilon. \]

warnings.title: Upper Bound
warnings.message: Extracted theorem/definition-like environment has no explicit title. The converter used a humanized label fallback, but the source should add an environment title for stable extraction and readable graph labels.
warnings.label: def:upper-bound
warnings.excerpt: Let $A \subseteq S$ and let $u \in S$. The element $u$ is called an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if \[ x \le u \qquad \text{for every } x \in A. \]

warnings.title: Lower Bound
warnings.message: Extracted theorem/definition-like environment has no explicit title. The converter used a humanized label fallback, but the source should add an environment title for stable extraction and readable graph labels.
warnings.label: def:lower-bound
warnings.excerpt: Let $A \subseteq S$ and let $\ell \in S$. The element $\ell$ is called a \emph{lower bound} of $A$ if \[ \ell \le x \qquad \text{for every } x \in A. \]

warnings.title: Minimum
warnings.message: Extracted theorem/definition-like environment has no explicit title. The converter used a humanized label fallback, but the source should add an environment title for stable extraction and readable graph labels.
warnings.label: def:minimum
warnings.excerpt: Let $A \subseteq S$ and let $m \in S$. The element $m$ is called a \emph{minimum} of $A$, denoted $m = \min A$, if $m \in A$ and \[ m \le x \qquad \text{for every } x \in A. \]

warnings.title: Maximum
warnings.message: Extracted theorem/definition-like environment has no explicit title. The converter used a humanized label fallback, but the source should add an environment title for stable extraction and readable graph labels.
warnings.label: def:maximum
warnings.excerpt: Let $A \subseteq S$ and let $m \in S$. The element $m$ is called a \emph{maximum} of $A$, denoted $m = \max A$, if $m \in A$ and \[ x \le m \qquad \text{for every } x \in A. \]

warnings.title: Supremum
warnings.message: Extracted theorem/definition-like environment has no explicit title. The converter used a humanized label fallback, but the source should add an environment title for stable extraction and readable graph labels.
warnings.label: def:supremum
warnings.excerpt: Let $A \subseteq S$ and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is called the \emph{supremum} of $A$, denoted $s = \sup A$, if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and $s$ is less than or equal to every upper bound of $A$.

warnings.title: Infimum
warnings.message: Extracted theorem/definition-like environment has no explicit title. The converter used a humanized label fallback, but the source should add an environment title for stable extraction and readable graph labels.
warnings.label: def:infimum
warnings.excerpt: Let $A \subseteq S$ and let $t \in S$. The element $t$ is called the \emph{infimum} of $A$, denoted $t = \inf A$, if $t$ is a lower bound of $A$ and every lower bound of $A$ is less than or equal to $t$.

warnings.title: Least Upper Bound Property
warnings.message: Extracted theorem/definition-like environment has no explicit title. The converter used a humanized label fallback, but the source should add an environment title for stable extraction and readable graph labels.
warnings.label: def:least-upper-bound-property
warnings.excerpt: Let $S$ be a partially ordered set. The set $S$ is said to have the \emph{Least Upper Bound Property} if every nonempty subset of $S$ that is bounded above in $S$ has a least upper bound in $S$.

warnings.title: Completeness Of Reals
warnings.message: Extracted theorem/definition-like environment has no explicit title. The converter used a humanized label fallback, but the source should add an environment title for stable extraction and readable graph labels.
warnings.label: ax:completeness-of-reals
warnings.excerpt: The ordered field $\mathbb{R}$ has the Least Upper Bound Property.

warnings.title: Dense Subset
warnings.message: Extracted theorem/definition-like environment has no explicit title. The converter used a humanized label fallback, but the source should add an environment title for stable extraction and readable graph labels.
warnings.label: def:dense-subset
warnings.excerpt: Let $D \subseteq S \subseteq \mathbb{R}$. The set $D$ is said to be \emph{dense in $S$} if for every $x \in S$ and every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $d \in D$ such that \[ |x-d|<\varepsilon. \]

warnings.title: Order Dense Subset
warnings.message: Extracted theorem/definition-like environment has no explicit title. The converter used a humanized label fallback, but the source should add an environment title for stable extraction and readable graph labels.
warnings.label: def:order-dense-subset
warnings.excerpt: Let $A \subseteq X$, where $X$ is a linearly ordered set. The subset $A$ is said to be \emph{order dense in $X$} if every nonempty open interval in $X$ contains a point of $A$.

warnings.title: Every Open Interval Contains Rational And Irrational
warnings.message: Extracted theorem/definition-like environment has no explicit title. The converter used a humanized label fallback, but the source should add an environment title for stable extraction and readable graph labels.
warnings.label: cor:every-open-interval-contains-rational-and-irrational
warnings.fallback_title: Every Open Interval Contains Rational And Irrational
warnings.excerpt: Every open interval in $\mathbb{R}$ contains both a rational number and an irrational number.
